an2.18:1.1: Then Ven. Ānanda went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, having bowed down to him, sat to one side. As he was sitting there, the Blessed One said to him,
an2.18:1.2: “I say categorically, Ānanda, that bodily misconduct, verbal misconduct, & mental misconduct should not be done.”
an2.18:1.3: “Given that the Blessed One has declared, lord, that bodily misconduct, verbal misconduct, & mental misconduct should not be done, what drawbacks can one expect when doing what should not be done?”
an2.18:1.4: “Given that I have declared, Ānanda, that bodily misconduct, verbal misconduct, & mental misconduct should not be done, these are the drawbacks one can expect when doing what should not be done:
an2.18:1.6: observant people, on close examination, criticize one;
an2.18:1.7: one’s bad reputation gets spread about;
an2.18:1.9: and—on the break-up of the body, after death—one reappears in a plane of deprivation, a bad destination, a lower realm, hell.
an2.18:1.10: Given that I have declared, Ānanda, that bodily misconduct, verbal misconduct, & mental misconduct should not be done, these are the drawbacks one can expect when doing what should not be done.
an2.18:2.1: “I say categorically, Ānanda, that good bodily conduct, good verbal conduct, & good mental conduct should be done.”
an2.18:2.2: “Given that the Blessed One has declared, lord, that good bodily conduct, good verbal conduct, & good mental conduct should be done, what rewards can one expect when doing what should be done?”
an2.18:2.3: “Given that I have declared, Ānanda, that good bodily conduct, good verbal conduct, & good mental conduct should be done, these are the rewards one can expect when doing what should be done:
an2.18:2.5: observant people, on close examination, praise one;
an2.18:2.6: one’s good reputation gets spread about;
an2.18:2.8: and—on the break-up of the body, after death—one reappears in a good destination, a heavenly world.
an2.18:2.9: Given that I have declared, Ānanda, that good bodily conduct, good verbal conduct, & good mental conduct should be done, these are the rewards one can expect when doing what should be done.”
an2.19:1.2: It is possible to abandon what is unskillful.
an2.19:1.3: If it were not possible to abandon what is unskillful, I would not say to you,
an2.19:1.5: But because it is possible to abandon what is unskillful, I say to you,
an2.19:1.7: If this abandoning of what is unskillful were conducive to harm and pain, I would not say to you,
an2.19:1.9: But because this abandoning of what is unskillful is conducive to benefit and pleasure, I say to you,
an2.19:2.2: It is possible to develop what is skillful.
an2.19:2.3: If it were not possible to develop what is skillful, I would not say to you,
an2.19:2.5: But because it is possible to develop what is skillful, I say to you,
an2.19:2.7: If this development of what is skillful were conducive to harm and pain, I would not say to you,
an2.19:2.9: But because this development of what is skillful is conducive to benefit and pleasure, I say to you,
